brightsmith_agentAttribution.reasoning: Gold promote of Regional Price Parities from base.bea_rpp (51 rows, 11 columns) to consumable.regional_price_parities (51 rows, 15 columns). Reads Silver via the Iceberg catalog, applies the Gold derivations (cost_tier via frozen 5-bucket CASE in src/gold/_cost_tier.py; four adjusted_Nk columns as round(N*1000*purchasing_power_multiplier, 2) for N in {30,50,75,100}), carries forward 8 Silver columns verbatim (state_fips, state_name, state_abbr, census_region, rpp_all_items, purchasing_power_multiplier, verification_status, data_year), stamps promoted_at at run time, and computes a deterministic record_id via compute_grain_id(['state_fips'], prefix='rpc'). The 'rpc' prefix is distinct from Silver's 'rpp' prefix so Gold and Silver record_ids cannot collide across zones (physical model requirement). Cost-tier breakpoints (>=108 very_high, [103,108) high, [97,103) average, [91,97) low, <91 very_low) are BT-106 governance-frozen — any change is a breaking change. Banker's rounding (Python round() == DuckDB ROUND()) is required for the DQ invariant abs(adjusted_Nk - round(N*1000*multiplier, 2)) <= 0.01 to hold on both sides of the engine boundary. Idempotent promote pattern: re-running produces 0 new rows. verification_status is carried forward from Silver per Bronze staff-review Condition 7 so downstream consumers (MCP tools, frontend salary display) see per-row provenance without joining back to Silver.
brightsmith_agentAttribution.reasoning_continued: No joins, no concept normalization, no cross-source work at this Gold hop — this is the smallest Gold transformation in the project. The entire hop is driven by base.bea_rpp plus the in-code cost-tier breakpoints.

documentation.description: Promote base.bea_rpp to consumable.regional_price_parities (Gold). Reads the 51-row Silver base table, derives cost_tier via the frozen 5-bucket CASE in src/gold/_cost_tier.py (BT-106 governance-frozen breakpoints), pre-computes four display-ready adjusted salary columns (adjusted_30k, adjusted_50k, adjusted_75k, adjusted_100k) using banker's rounding to match DuckDB's default ROUND() behavior, carries forward 8 Silver columns verbatim including verification_status (per Bronze Condition 7), stamps promoted_at at UTC now(), computes a deterministic record_id via compute_grain_id(row, ['state_fips'], prefix='rpc'), and writes to consumable.regional_price_parities via the Brightsmith idempotent promote pattern (append with dedup on record_id).

fields.name: record_id
fields.description: Silver grain id (prefix='rpp') — NOT carried to Gold; Gold computes its own rpc-prefixed record_id

fields.name: state_fips
fields.description: 2-digit zero-padded state FIPS code; grain field

fields.name: state_abbr
fields.description: USPS 2-letter abbreviation derived in Silver from FIPS_TO_USPS

fields.name: census_region
fields.description: U.S. Census region (Northeast|Midwest|South|West) derived in Silver

fields.name: rpp_all_items
fields.description: Regional Price Parity all-items index, national=100 scale

fields.name: verification_status
fields.description: Per-row provenance: bea_official (8 states) | estimate (43 states)

fields.name: data_year
fields.description: Vintage year of the RPP estimate (2024)

fields.name: source_load_date
fields.description: Bronze load date — NOT carried to Gold

fields.name: ingested_at
fields.description: Silver promote timestamp — NOT carried to Gold; replaced by Gold promoted_at

documentation.description: In-code frozen cost-tier classifier loaded by the Gold transformer. Provides COST_TIER_BREAKPOINTS, a 4-entry descending tuple of (threshold, CostTier) pairs: (108.0, very_high), (103.0, high), (97.0, average), (91.0, low); values below 91.0 fall through to very_low. Implements left-closed interval semantics [lo, hi) via top-down evaluation. The 5-bucket enum and its breakpoints are BT-106 governance-frozen — any change is a breaking change per the physical model (governance/models/gold-regional-price-parities-physical.md) and requires a new spec. Treated as a proper lineage node so cost_tier has a named, traceable provenance rather than being opaque in-code logic.

fields.name: COST_TIER_BREAKPOINTS
fields.description: Frozen descending breakpoint list: ((108.0,'very_high'),(103.0,'high'),(97.0,'average'),(91.0,'low')); fallthrough to 'very_low'

fields.name: CostTier
fields.description: 5-bucket enum: very_high | high | average | low | very_low

fields.name: cost_tier
fields.transformation: DERIVED via classify_cost_tier(rpp_all_items) — frozen 5-bucket CASE in src/gold/_cost_tier.py

fields.name: verification_status
fields.transformation: DIRECT passthrough from base.bea_rpp.verification_status (per Bronze Condition 7)

record_id.transformationDescription: Deterministic grain id computed by compute_grain_id(row, ['state_fips'], prefix='rpc'). The 'rpc' (regional-price-consumable) prefix is intentionally distinct from Silver's 'rpp' prefix so Gold and Silver record_ids cannot collide across zones — this is a physical-model requirement. Provides the stable natural key for the idempotent promote pattern's dedup step.

state_fips.transformationDescription: Identity passthrough from base.bea_rpp.state_fips via SILVER_PASSTHROUGH_FIELDS in derive_gold_rows(). No validation, normalization, or relabeling — Silver has already enforced the ^\d{2}$ regex and the 51-entry FIPS_TO_USPS membership check.

state_name.transformationDescription: Identity passthrough from base.bea_rpp.state_name via SILVER_PASSTHROUGH_FIELDS. Silver has already stripped whitespace and enforced non-empty.

state_abbr.transformationDescription: Identity passthrough from base.bea_rpp.state_abbr via SILVER_PASSTHROUGH_FIELDS. The value was derived at Silver via FIPS_TO_USPS lookup; Gold does not re-derive or re-validate it.

census_region.transformationDescription: Identity passthrough from base.bea_rpp.census_region via SILVER_PASSTHROUGH_FIELDS. The value was derived at Silver via FIPS_TO_CENSUS_REGION lookup (DC documented quirk: 'South' per U.S. Census convention); Gold does not re-derive it.

rpp_all_items.transformationDescription: Identity passthrough from base.bea_rpp.rpp_all_items via SILVER_PASSTHROUGH_FIELDS. Value remains on the BEA national=100 index scale. Also read (separately, in the same row) as the input to cost_tier classification.

purchasing_power_multiplier.transformationDescription: Identity passthrough from base.bea_rpp.purchasing_power_multiplier via SILVER_PASSTHROUGH_FIELDS. The value (100.0 / rpp_all_items) was pre-computed at Silver as the single source of truth; Gold reads it verbatim and also uses it (separately) as the multiplier input for the four adjusted_Nk columns. Carrying it through lets MCP and frontend consumers read the canonical value without recomputing.

cost_tier.transformationDescription: classify_cost_tier(rpp_all_items) evaluates the frozen 4-entry COST_TIER_BREAKPOINTS tuple top-down with left-closed interval semantics [lo, hi): rpp >= 108.0 -> 'very_high'; 103.0 <= rpp < 108.0 -> 'high'; 97.0 <= rpp < 103.0 -> 'average'; 91.0 <= rpp < 97.0 -> 'low'; rpp < 91.0 -> 'very_low'. Breakpoints and enum values are BT-106 governance-frozen per governance/models/gold-regional-price-parities-physical.md — any change is a breaking change requiring a new spec. Implemented in src/gold/_cost_tier.py as a string-valued Enum for direct VARCHAR comparability without coercion.

adjusted_30k.transformationDescription: compute_adjusted_salary(30000.0, purchasing_power_multiplier) = round(30000 * purchasing_power_multiplier, 2). Uses Python's built-in round() which implements IEEE 754 round-half-to-even (banker's rounding) to match DuckDB's default ROUND() behavior. Consistency across transformer, DQ engine, and database is required so the invariant abs(adjusted_30k - round(30000 * multiplier, 2)) <= 0.01 holds on both sides. Frozen salary anchor in SALARY_ANCHORS; a future spec adding e.g. adjusted_150k must append to SALARY_ANCHORS and append a column to the Gold schema.

adjusted_50k.transformationDescription: compute_adjusted_salary(50000.0, purchasing_power_multiplier) = round(50000 * purchasing_power_multiplier, 2). Banker's rounding per IEEE 754 round-half-to-even to match DuckDB's default ROUND(). Frozen salary anchor in SALARY_ANCHORS.

adjusted_75k.transformationDescription: compute_adjusted_salary(75000.0, purchasing_power_multiplier) = round(75000 * purchasing_power_multiplier, 2). Banker's rounding per IEEE 754 round-half-to-even to match DuckDB's default ROUND(). Frozen salary anchor in SALARY_ANCHORS.

adjusted_100k.transformationDescription: compute_adjusted_salary(100000.0, purchasing_power_multiplier) = round(100000 * purchasing_power_multiplier, 2). Banker's rounding per IEEE 754 round-half-to-even to match DuckDB's default ROUND(). Frozen salary anchor in SALARY_ANCHORS.

verification_status.transformationDescription: Identity passthrough from base.bea_rpp.verification_status via SILVER_PASSTHROUGH_FIELDS. Carries per-row provenance ('bea_official' for the 8 BEA-verified states; 'estimate' for the other 43) forward to Gold per Bronze staff-review Condition 7 so downstream consumers (MCP tools, frontend salary-adjustment display) surface provenance without joining back to Silver. When the live BEA API refresh lands post-hackathon, every row flips to 'bea_official' at Silver and propagates verbatim through Gold with no schema change.

data_year.transformationDescription: Identity passthrough from base.bea_rpp.data_year via SILVER_PASSTHROUGH_FIELDS. Every row in the 2026-04-10 vintage is 2024, enforced by inherited P0 DQ invariants. Single-vintage supersession-by-replacement contract propagates from raw-ingest-bea-rpp through Silver to Gold.

promoted_at.transformationDescription: Framework-injected: Gold promotion UTC timestamp stamped by add_record_ids(gold_rows, promoted_at) where promoted_at = datetime.datetime.now(tz=datetime.timezone.utc) at transform() call time. Distinct from Silver's ingested_at (which is not carried forward). Tests pass a fixed value for determinism.
